definition: Food CPI weight ranges from 8% (US) to 49% (Nigeria), creating an arithmetic mechanism where identical food price shocks produce fundamentally different inflation outcomes. Countries with highest food CPI weights are projected to add ~800M people by 2050, creating a self-reinforcing loop: more people, more demand, tighter markets, higher food prices, larger CPI impact, greater instability.
evidence: 20% wheat price increase = ~0.3pp US CPI vs ~2.5pp Egypt CPI
Countries with highest food CPI weights (Nigeria, Ethiopia, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Egypt) projected to add ~800M people by 2050
Self-reinforcing loop mechanism is arithmetic, not a model
open_questions: How do substitution effects moderate the arithmetic impact in practice?
At what threshold does food inflation trigger political instability feedback loops?
